blurb:
She came in from animal control with no resume and no fanfare, just a medium-sized Husky-Basset mix named Shellie with a whole lot of heart and a surprisingly impressive skill set. Around five years old and about 32 pounds, she graduated from the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups immersive training program, which means she already knows sit, stay, down, come, drop it, leave it, and place. She walks well on a leash, uses proper door etiquette, and handles baths and nail trims without drama. For a dog who came in from the shelter system, she is remarkably put-together.

Shellie loves people. That is her whole thing. She gets excited when she sees other dogs or people, and she can be vocal about it, but she settles once she gets a few visits with the same dog and figures out they are friendly. She startles at fast movements or loud noises but recovers quickly. That is worth knowing going in, but it hasn't slowed her down at all.

She'd do best in a home without toddlers and with kids who understand not to hug her since she doesn't love feeling restrained. If you have a calm, patient dog at home already, bring them in for a meet and greet. She warms up fast once she decides you're her people, and once she does, she's all in.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. You can fill out an adoption inquiry on their website or stop by during open hours to meet her.
shelter_address: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue, 2523 Broad Street, Kennesaw, GA 30144
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current adoption hours
scoring_notes:
• Remarkable skill set from Prison Pups program makes her one of the most adoption-ready dogs in the shelter
• Husky-Basset mix with a people-first personality offers broad household appeal and easy reader connection
• Animal control intake with no prior home suggests meaningful time in the system, adding quiet urgency to her story

blurb:
Thirty-two pounds of personality with a soundtrack to match. Sky is a three-year-old Pit Bull and French Bulldog mix who makes little grunty noises and, according to everyone who's met her, is always ready for affection. Always. She's dainty for a pittie mix, but she more than makes up for it in presence.

She's heartworm negative, fully vaccinated, spayed, microchipped, and flea-treated, so she's ready to go. She gets along with other dogs, which opens up a lot of options. Her history with kids and housetraining are both unknowns since she came in without much background, but neither of those is a dealbreaker. A meet and greet at the shelter can answer a lot of questions before you commit.

If you want a small dog with a big, expressive personality who is going to make you laugh on a regular basis, Sky is worth the trip. Lewisville Animal Services is on College Pkwy in Lewisville, TX. Stop by during visiting hours to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Heartworm-negative, fully cleared, and dog-friendly — the cleanest adoption profile in the dog group
• Grunty noises and oversized personality in a 32-pound frame is an irresistible visual hook for any newsletter
• Mid-range listing age with unknowns on kids and housetraining suggests she's been quietly passed over despite being highly adoptable

blurb:
Panda Bear is a five-year-old American Shorthair mix who just arrived at the shelter, so her full personality is still being figured out. What they do know is that she came from a home with multiple other animals and handled it just fine. That's actually a big deal. It means she's not going to be thrown off by a busy household.

She weighs just over 11 pounds, she's litter box trained, and she's already vaccinated, spayed, and microchipped. Beyond that, she's still settling in and showing the shelter staff who she is. Sometimes the cats with shorter bios just need a home to open up in.

If you're looking for a five-year-old cat who has already proven she can share space with other animals and just needs somewhere quiet to land, Panda Bear is worth going to meet.

She's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Parkway. Walk in during visiting hours to introduce yourself.
scoring_notes:
• Proven multi-pet household history makes her an easy yes for the largest adopter demographic
• Just-arrived status means newsletter feature could drive an early adoption before she gets overlooked
• Fully vetted at five years old — a mature, ready-to-go cat with zero medical surprises

blurb:
His name is Porkchop and yes, it's perfect. This three-year-old domestic shorthair came in as an outdoor cat, which means he's seen some things, done some things, and is now fully ready to retire indoors. He weighs 12 pounds, which feels right for a cat named Porkchop.

He's a little hesitant in new situations. Not unfriendly, just the kind of cat who needs to take stock before he decides he likes you. Give him a calm, quiet home and some patience and he's going to settle in and become exactly the low-drama companion you didn't know you needed. He's litter box trained, fully vaccinated, neutered, and microchipped.

They don't have info yet on how he does with kids or other animals since his background is mostly outdoors. If that matters for your household, you can stop by and see how he does in person before you commit.

Porkchop is at Lewisville Animal Services on College Parkway. Walk-ins are welcome during visiting hours.
scoring_notes:
• Name alone is a newsletter headline — instant reader engagement and shareability
• Reformed outdoor cat narrative gives readers a satisfying 'retirement' arc to root for
• Fully vetted with walk-in access lowers every barrier to same-week adoption

blurb:
Tony is a 13-year-old Boston Terrier who lost his owner and ended up at Mostly Mutts. That's a hard way to start over. But if you spend five minutes reading about him, you'll understand why his foster family is so clearly rooting for him to land somewhere good.

His foster describes him as an absolute lovebug, and that tracks. He follows his people around all day, shares dog beds with the other dogs in the house, and pretty much ignores the cats (except when there's food nearby, but honestly, same). He had eye removal surgery recently and is deaf, so he's working with some limitations, but none of that seems to slow him down much. He's a shadow, not a handful.

He does best with a stay-at-home or work-from-home person who can be around most of the day. He gets a little vocal when left alone, which is understandable given everything he's been through. He's not destructive, just attached. If you work from home and have been thinking about a calm, older dog who just wants to be near you, Tony is worth a visit. Kids in the home should be old enough to be gentle and mindful of his limited sight and hearing.

Tony is currently in a foster home and available through Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue in Kennesaw. Submit an inquiry through their website or call to set up a meet.
scoring_notes:
• 13-year-old who lost his owner, survived eye removal surgery, and is deaf — emotionally the most compelling story in the entire batch
• Stay-at-home requirement and senior status limit his pool significantly, making newsletter visibility genuinely critical to placement
• Foster family's visible emotional investment signals a dog readers will root for — high shareability and donation-trigger potential
